title: Smoky Chewy Turkish Flour Manti
description: A traditional Turkish dish featuring smoky and chewy manti filled with vegetables, made with white flour and katsuramuki-cut, perfect for a festive meal. This vegetarian-friendly dish is a delight to eat.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and salt, then gradually add the water and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each piece into a thin circle.
In a separate pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic until softened.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the pan and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the smoked paprika and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Place a tablespoon of the mushroom mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal the manti.
Steam the manti over boiling water for 10-15 minutes, or until they are cooked through and slightly puffed.
Serve the smoky chewy Turkish flour manti hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
